summary.intent: Implement Checkbox adapter components for both Leptos (#320) and Dioxus (#431) frameworks, then iteratively fix browser review issues and harden the adapter delivery workflow documentation around outcome parity.
summary.outcome: Checkbox adapters delivered with SSR/unit, wasm browser, E2E, and widget coverage across both frameworks and all six widget crates; multiple browser-review UI fixes applied; a separate docs-only PR created hardening the adapter outcome parity workflow with playwright-cli guidance and a counterpart fallback matrix.

repo: The adapter delivery workflow requires counterpart UX brief, parity matrix, E2E coverage, widgets in all six crates, and wasm browser tests in the same PR — not just the component files.
`cargo xtask spec issue-deps` validates that native GitHub dependency edges match issue bodies and reports any missing or extra dependencies.

The adapter parity lint (`cargo xtask lint adapter-parity`) only discovers `test_*.rs` files, not the repo's actual `checkbox.rs` naming convention; a thin `test_checkbox.rs` smoke file is needed to register coverage.

Core Checkbox events are `SetValue`, `SetProps`, `SetHasDescription`, `Toggle`, `Focus`, `Blur`, `Check`, `Uncheck`, `Reset` — the adapter specs still contained stale `SetDisabled`/`SetChecked`-style names that had to be corrected.
The `ars-sr-input` CSS class already exists for visually hiding native form inputs; `hidden_input_attrs()` was not emitting it for Checkbox.
Field/Form/Fieldset agnostic core (#206) is closed but adapter tasks #332 (Leptos) and #423 (Dioxus) are still open — full validation-context integration in Checkbox depends on those.
Dioxus RSX requires all element attributes to appear before child nodes.
The workspace lint denies `unused_qualifications`, so RSX expansions inside test files can trigger lint failures.

code.finding: `with_api_snapshot` is read-only by design; calling API helper methods that internally send machine events from within it causes a panic. Event dispatch must use direct `machine.send(Event::*)` calls outside the snapshot closure.

`hidden_input_attrs()` was not emitting `class='ars-sr-input'`, causing the native form input to render visibly in widget demos. Adding the class visually hides the input while keeping it form-participatory.
Reactive attrs must be derived through memos tied to machine state rather than one-shot `with_api_snapshot` calls, otherwise state changes after interactions are not reflected in the DOM.
Dioxus boolean attrs serialize as the string `'true'` rather than an empty string like HTML; E2E and wasm assertions must check for `'true'` rather than attribute presence.
Leptos wasm test cleanup uses drop semantics on the mount handle, not a `.unmount()` method call.
WebDriver `send_keys` and direct `.click()` fail on custom non-native controls in E2E; interaction must go through DOM `KeyboardEvent`/`PointerEvent` dispatch via `execute_script`.

workflow: The TDD cycle here was genuinely valuable: writing failing SSR tests first exposed the missing module before any implementation, and wasm tests caught the `with_api_snapshot` event-dispatch bug that SSR could not see.
`playwright-cli run-code 'async page => ...'` is the correct syntax for complex DOM evaluations; the simpler `eval` wrapper does not accept async page functions.
Use separate named Playwright sessions (`-s=reference`, `-s=local`) to compare counterpart and local pages without losing state between commands.
E2E fixture servers need localhost binding, which is blocked by the sandbox; those gates must be run outside the sandbox.
Dioxus optional props (`description`, `error_message`) passed as `None` in RSX must use `None::<ViewFn>` or the equivalent typed None to avoid ambiguous inference.
Leptos optional macro props take the inner value directly at call sites, not `Some(value)` — the macro wraps it automatically.
Snapshot files from `cargo insta` accumulate as `.snap.new` when tests fail; `cargo insta accept` must be run to promote them after intentional anatomy changes.

When measuring rendered dimensions with Playwright, elements may have zero bounding boxes if they are in the DOM but hidden under a non-visible tab; scrolling or tab-switching is required before querying layout.

friction: The `with_api_snapshot` read-only constraint is not surfaced at compile time — it panics at runtime during wasm tests, requiring a full browser-target rebuild to discover.
The adapter parity lint only counts `test_*.rs` files, silently reporting Checkbox as uncovered even after real tests were added under the repo's actual naming convention.
Sandbox blocked the E2E fixture server from binding localhost, requiring a separate escalation path to run browser tests.
Playwright session cache creation under `~/Library/Caches/ms-playwright` required user approval mid-session.
`playwright-cli eval` and `run-code` have different invocation shapes that are not obvious from the skill doc alone.
The adapter specs contained stale event names (`SetDisabled`, `SetChecked`) that diverged from the actual core API, requiring a spec correction pass after implementation.
Dioxus wasm tests needed `#[allow(unused_qualifications)]` suppression because RSX macro expansions triggered the workspace lint.
open_items: Hover and press interaction state (`data-ars-hovered`, `data-ars-pressed`) not yet wired into the Checkbox adapter — foundations exist in `ars-interactions` but Checkbox does not consume them.
Field/Form/Fieldset adapter delivery (#332 Leptos, #423 Dioxus) is a prerequisite for full validation-context integration in Checkbox and all future input adapters.

Checkbox indeterminate semantics need an explicit spec decision: visual/presentational state vs. machine value state (relevant to how `isIndeterminate` in React Aria differs from the current tri-state model).
Custom validation / `validationBehavior` API on Checkbox deferred until Field/Form adapters exist.
`cargo xci-fast` intentionally not run — deferred until user approves the full CI run.
Checkbox implementation changes on `codex/issue-320-431-checkbox-adapters` are not yet committed or pushed to a PR — the docs PR was created separately first.
Checklist items for hover/pressed and field-composition axes will remain incomplete in Checkbox parity matrix until those foundations are delivered.
